title: SRS — Stimulated Raman Scattering microscopy
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Freudiger et al. 2008 Science SRS imaging; Saar et al. 2010 video-rate SRS; Cheng-Xie 2015 comparison; Ji et al. 2013 SRS brain.

E.description: Stimulated Raman scattering uses two synchronized pulses (pump at omega_p, Stokes at omega_s) with Omega = omega_p - omega_s matching a vibrational mode; the pump experiences stimulated loss (SRL) while Stokes gains (SRG), both proportional to Im[chi^(3)]. Detection via high-frequency lock-in amplification (>1 MHz) of modulated Stokes/pump gives SNR bounded by laser shot noise only (no non-resonant background).
E.world_state_x: Concentration map N(r) (moieties/volume) and vibrational cross-section sigma_SRS(Omega)
E.observation_y: Lock-in demodulated pump-loss signal y(r, Omega)
physical_parameters_theta: Pump/Stokes wavelengths (lambda_p ~ 800 nm, lambda_s ~ 1040 nm typical)
Pulse duration (ps for narrow-band, fs for spectral-focusing)
Thermal lensing and cross-phase modulation artifacts

W.regime: SRS is linear in concentration, unlike CARS (|chi|^2). No non-resonant background. Signal is shot-noise limited; SNR scales as sqrt(I_p * I_s * tau).

C.solver_class: Linear regression (CLS), hyperspectral deconvolution, spectral-focusing chirp decoding, learned denoisers
C.error_bound: N_hat uncertainty ~ sigma_shot / sqrt(tau * I_p * I_s * sigma_SRS^2)
